message:
fix(types): accept legacy "str"/"string" serde aliases for Utf8 DType

Restores deserialization compatibility with conformance fixtures
and external oracle payloads that predate the DType::Utf8 rename.
Without these aliases, any fixture authored before the Utf8 change
would fail to deserialize with "unknown variant: str" / "string".

crates/fp-types/src/lib.rs:
 - DType::Utf8 now carries `#[serde(alias = "string", alias = "str")]`
 - Scalar::Utf8 same treatment
 - Two new tests locking in the behaviour:
   * `dtype_utf8_deserializes_legacy_aliases` covers the enum variant
   * `scalar_utf8_deserializes_legacy_aliases` covers the tagged
     enum payload form (`{"kind":"str","value":"x"}`)

crates/fp-types/Cargo.toml:
 - Adds serde_json to [dev-dependencies] since the new tests
   parse JSON directly.

Cargo.lock: updated fp-types dependency list to record the new
dev-dependency edge.
